title: Phymatopsis hastata
taxonomy: Phymatopsis hastata belongs to the family Blechnaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as rock ferns or chain ferns. This plant is classified within the genus Phymatopsis, a monotypic genus containing only the single species P. hastata. The family Blechnaceae is further classified within the order Polypodiales, which is a subgroup of ferns. Phymatopsis hastata is native to Australia, where it is found growing in a variety of habitats. This species is considered to be an endangered plant due to the destruction of its habitats.
